description: Machine-readable schema for the Event Script flow DSL (Rust port of mercury-composable; the flow YAML syntax is identical to the Java engine's — flow files port unchanged). Mirrors flow-grammar.md. An AI agent should ingest this to author and validate flow YAML deterministically.

flow_structure.meaning: unique flow id; referenced by rest.yaml and as flow://{id}
entry-point task (route name or task name)
route or flow://id; required if any task uses ext: namespace
list of task definitions; must include >=1 with execution: end

task_fields.meaning: function route or flow://{flow-id}; process or name required
unique task id; defaults to process; required only if the same process is used >1 time
list of 'source -> target' mappings ([] for none)
list of 'source -> target' mappings ([] for none)
next task(s); shaped by execution type
ordered task list; required for pipeline
model.* list to iterate for a dynamic fork
ms (int) or model.* var; must be < flow.ttl

execution_types.does: run, then the one next task
branch on the decision value (true->next[0]/false->next[1]; or numeric 1-indexed, multi-way)
fan out to all next tasks concurrently
run next tasks concurrently, resume at join; optional source to iterate
run pipeline tasks in order (optional loop), then the one next task
send the HTTP response now, then continue async to next
terminate; output mappings form the final response

data_mapping.rule: 'source -> target' (one -> per rule; three-part 'LHS -> model.var -> RHS' allowed)

conversions_deprecated.note: the legacy 'simple type matching' source suffixes still parse for backward compatibility but are DEPRECATED — do not generate them in new flows; shape values in the function itself or use an f: simple-plugin function

data_mapping.input_target_note: in an input mapping the RHS with no namespace is the function's input body key (e.g. 'model.order -> order'); use header.* for input headers
data_mapping.output_source_note: on the LHS of an output mapping, 'result' (bare) is the whole function result and 'result.x' a field; model.* always needs a specific key (no whole-model access)

references: first.task, next, join, and pipeline reference a task by its name, which defaults to process

deployment.flows_folder: the application's resources/flows/ (an app is a standalone examples/<name>/ workspace crate with resources/ beside its Cargo.toml)
deployment.functions: flow tasks call composable functions by route name — Rust authoring contract in guides/event-driven/ai-agent-guide.md

invariants: flow.id, flow.description, flow.ttl, first.task, tasks all present; ttl >= 1s
every task has description, input, output, and a valid execution
there is >=1 task with execution: end
decision needs >=2 next; sequential/response/pipeline need exactly 1; end/sink have no next
fork requires join; pipeline requires a pipeline list
a name is required when the same process appears in more than one task
ext: targets require external.state.machine to be declared
